title: commiphora baluensis
intro: Commiphora baluensis is a species of tree in the family Burseraceae, native to Borneo and known for its fragrant resin and medicinal properties. The resin of Commiphora baluensis contains oleoresin, which is rich in boswellic acids, providing anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, bronchitis, and skin infections, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Commiphora baluensis can be prepared as a herbal remedy through processes such as steaming, distillation, and extraction, resulting in products like essential oils, ointments, and capsules.
